title: rondeletia pedicellaris
intro: Rondeletia pedicellaris, also known as the Florida rosemallow, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Rondeletia pedicellaris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
